answer_text: I am committed to working with the Minister for Education and other Executive colleagues to implement the Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.I am aware that the childcare sector is facing challenges, including the recruitment and retention of staff.  My department is working with the Department of Education, (DE) the Department of Health, (DoH) and the Department for Communities (DfC) to consider a range of interventions to support this sector.DE has responsibility for the targeted business support scheme for childcare providers. However, as my department has expertise in delivering business support, my officials are assisting with this work. This includes an assessment of the relevant labour to develop a clearer picture of the type of supports that might be required.  This exercise will involve direct engagement with childcare providers to gather insights into the challenges they face, the key issues impacting the financial sustainability of providers and options for a government intervention. In the immediate term, I have committed £740,000 of my Skills Fund to support pathways into the childcare sector, helping the supply of workers into this critically important sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any actions he is taking to support the delivery of the Strategic Framework to End Violence Against Women and Girls.
answer_text: Addressing violence against women and girls is an issue for all areas of society and education has a role in supporting that. The Delivery Plan brings into sharp focus my Department's responsibilities, and we will consider how the curriculum can support the wider strategy, with schools having an important role to play in addressing violence against women and girls. My Department will contribute to the Delivery Plan and is establishing an Oversight Group to decide how the actions it is required to take forward will be delivered together with key delivery partners including the Executive Office.  This will include engagement with stakeholders.  Officials will bring forward recommendations for my consideration. Delivery of comprehensive, age-appropriate and developmentally appropriate Relationships and Sexuality Education is an important part in reducing violence against women and girls. Our curriculum is designed to provide opportunities to educate young people to develop healthy relationships.  Pupils have the opportunity to explore their management of a range of feelings and emotions and those of others; initiating, developing and sustaining mutually satisfying relationships, explore the qualities of a loving, respectful relationship and develop coping strategies to deal with challenging relationship scenarios.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions he has had with the Minister of Education in relation to improving the levels of physical activity of school children, so they reach the recommended level of one hour per day.
answer_text: I have had no discussions with Minister Givan on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the Northern Ireland Public Services Ombudsman's report on an Investigation of a Complaint Against the Western Health and Social Care Trust, published on 23 October 2024.
answer_text: The recent Northern Ireland Public Services Ombudsman's (NIPSO) report was primarily focussed on a complaint against the Western Health and Social Care Trust.  However, I have considered the findings and recommendations which are relevant to my department in relation to the use of restrictive practices in schools.    My department has consulted on draft statutory guidance on the Reduction and Management of Restrictive Practices in Educational Settings. Responses to this consultation have been analysed and officials are currently working with stakeholders to revise and refine the draft guidance, reflecting on the issues raised during the consultation process.  As part of this work, the findings and recommendations of the Ombudsman’s report will be reviewed, in collaboration with health officials and in the context of the draft guidance for schools.  Departmental officials have scheduled a meeting with NIPSO officials in the coming weeks to discuss the issues raised in the report.  Departmental officials will continue to work collaboratively with stakeholders as work progresses to finalise the guidance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of whether the delivery of comprehensive, age-appropriate and developmentally-appropriate Relationships and Sexuality Education is an essential part in reducing violence against women and girls.
answer_text: Addressing violence against women and girls is an issue for all areas of society and education has a role in supporting that. The Delivery Plan brings into sharp focus my Department's responsibilities, and we will consider how the curriculum can support the wider strategy, with schools having an important role to play in addressing violence against women and girls. My Department will contribute to the Delivery Plan and is establishing an Oversight Group to decide how the actions it is required to take forward will be delivered together with key delivery partners including the Executive Office.  This will include engagement with stakeholders.  Officials will bring forward recommendations for my consideration. Delivery of comprehensive, age-appropriate and developmentally appropriate Relationships and Sexuality Education is an important part in reducing violence against women and girls. Our curriculum is designed to provide opportunities to educate young people to develop healthy relationships.  Pupils have the opportunity to explore their management of a range of feelings and emotions and those of others; initiating, developing and sustaining mutually satisfying relationships, explore the qualities of a loving, respectful relationship and develop coping strategies to deal with challenging relationship scenarios.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 16715/22-27, whether the Schools Trauma Advisory Referral Service is currently not accepting referrals.
answer_text: The Schools Trauma Advisory Referral Service (STARS) was available to schools with children seeking sanctuary for early advice and onwards referrals to the Educational Psychology and other relevant Education Authority (EA) services that support the emotional health and wellbeing of children.Due to a shortage in the availability of Educational Psychologist support within the EA, STARS is not currently taking referrals. The Intercultural Education Service (IES) are currently working with EA colleagues to explore alternative ways of providing trauma-informed training, advice and support to schools who require urgent early intervention for children seeking sanctuary.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 16717/22-27, to provide a comprehensive breakdown of the £25 million.
answer_text: My Written Statement of 23 May 2024 set out the breakdown of the £25m allocated to Early Learning and Childcare for 2024/25. The full statement is available here: Written Ministerial Statement - Early Learning and Childcare Measures 2024/25 | Department of Education.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the budget allocation for 2023-24 for the Maze/Long Kesh Development Corporation.
answer_text: The budget allocation for the Maze Long Kesh Development Corporation for 2023/24 is set out below.  Resource  £1,069kReceipts  £(108k)*Capital  £936kCapital Receipts  £(11k)**Figures shown in brackets represent income.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) for his assessment of the proposals in the Independent Review of Education to expand Sure Start provision; and (ii) whether this proposal has been costed.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the timeline for (i) when the Maze/Long Kesh Development Corporation business plan will be signed off; and (ii) when a plan for the future direction of the site will be published.
answer_text: The Board of the Maze/Long Kesh Development Corporation met with the TEO Permanent Secretary on 27 February 2025.  Since that meeting, we have received advice from officials on the options for the board and the immediate priorities. This is currently under consideration. No decisions have yet been made on the future of the site. The Maze/Long Kesh Development Corporation revised business plan was approved on 15 April 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when his Department will publish bullying data and trends captured by schools, as required through the Addressing Bullying in Schools Act (Northern Ireland) 2016.
answer_text: Under the Addressing Bullying in Schools Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 (the Act), a school’s Board of Governors is responsible for the implementation of the Act. Schools are required to record data on incidents or alleged incidents of bullying type behaviour, their motivation and their outcome. The legislation does not include the requirement for schools to share the data that they record with the Department.The 2024 Schools Omnibus Survey will include questions in relation to addressing bullying type behaviour in schools and compliance with the Act. There are also questions on bullying type behaviour, pastoral care and positive relationships being piloted this term (September – December 2024) as part of Education and Training Inspectorate (ETI) inspections.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the funding provided by his Department to each Sure Start in each of the last five years.

To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) any actions he is considering to improve pay for childcare workers; and (ii) any engagement he has had with the Minister for the Economy in relation to this issue.
answer_text: The challenges facing the childcare workforce, including recruitment, retention, qualification levels and rates of pay are being considered as part of the development of the Executive Early Learning and Childcare Strategy.  In conjunction with my Department, officials from the Department for the Economy, the Department of Health and the Department for Communities are exploring what actions might be possible in the context of a sector largely comprising private childcare businesses and self- employed childminders.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the recommendation for the establishment of a single authority for all schools at point 8.50 in the Independent Review of Education.
answer_text: As reported in the Independent Review of Education, the current school management arrangements are complex and cumbersome and I support the panel’s recommendation that they need to be reformed. The Report highlighted the suboptimal support for controlled schools and this is a key priority that I wish to address. A controlled schools Taskforce has recently been established with the aim to improve effectiveness in the short-term and plan for the creation of a managing authority for controlled schools. This does not preclude work to better support other sectors in future and, under any model, I expect that managing authorities should work together to maximise consistency.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) when the childcare strategy will be published; and (ii) the reason for its delay.
answer_text: It is my intention to have a draft Early Learning and Childcare (ELC) Strategy ready for Executive consideration and public consultation in 2025.   As agreed with Executive colleagues in May 2024, implementing the package of ELC measures in 2024/25 has been given priority over the past 6 months. Work on requirements for 2025/26 and the longer-term strategy has now recommenced.The development of the strategy will be informed by evaluation of the measures being implemented in 2024/25 and new data emerging, including from a NISRA parental survey I have commissioned, the results of which will be available by April 2025.   Further stakeholder engagement is also planned on key issues such as workforce challenges and accessibility of provision.Given the level of investment likely to be required and sector workforce capacity constraints, it will take a number of years to deliver on all the commitments in the Strategy, once agreed.  The scale and pace of implementation will largely depend on the budget made available by the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when he will repeal Article 4 (1) (c) of the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1998.
answer_text: A public consultation on draft statutory guidance on the Reduction and Management of Restrictive Practices in Educational Settings closed on the 10 November 2023.  I have considered analysis from the responses to the consultation and asked officials to work with stakeholders to revise and refine the draft guidance, reflecting on the issues raised.  The need to repeal Article 4 (1) (c) of the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1998 will be considered in the context of the revised guidance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when updated statutory guidance on restrictive practices will be published.
answer_text: A public consultation on draft statutory guidance on the Reduction and Management of Restrictive Practices in Educational Settings closed on the 10 November 2023.  I have now considered analysis from the responses to the consultation and asked officials to work with stakeholders to revise and refine the draft guidance, reflecting on the issues raised.  This is a challenging and complex issue and there are many diverging opinions which need to be carefully considered. Subject to timely progress it is anticipated that statutory guidance will be published in the 2024/25 school year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether street lights can be installed along Station Road, Moira, given that it connects the train station to the town and the substantial traffic levels on the road.
answer_text: Officials in my department have advised that they have been working with Translink to address a number of capacity concerns at Moira Station and as such a section of footway from the Motorway Bridge to Moira Station that was previously widened to accommodate commuters and pedestrians has recently been extended by approximately 110m from the motorway bridge towards Moira. Street lighting is currently situated at either side of the train station, in the direction of Glenavy as far as the Chestnut Hill Road junction and in the direction of Moira as far as the bridge near Ballycanal Holiday homes. Locations beyond these, which include Station Road, are rural in nature and do not meet the current minimum criteria for the provision of street lighting. My Department’s street lighting policy helps to ensure lighting is provided where it is most needed as well as managing future maintenance and energy costs and the environmental impacts of light pollution.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQO 922/22-27, when his officials will report on the way forward.
answer_text: As previously stated, my officials are considering the most effective way forward on this issue.  I hope to be in a position to make a further announcement in the near future.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) for his assessment of the 14 - 19 Framework within the NI Audit Office report Developing the skills for Northern Ireland's future; and (ii) to detail any action he is taking following this review.
answer_text: My Department is jointly responsible for delivery of the 14-19 Education and Training Framework along with the Department for the Economy.As reflected in the Northern Ireland Audit Office report on the Department for Economy’s strategy ‘Developing the skills for Northern Ireland’s future’ work has already progressed to address issues identified in the governance and implementation of the 14-19 Framework.It would be inappropriate to comment any further in detail in advance of a Public Accounts Committee (PAC) hearing on these issues scheduled for 21 November, the PAC’s subsequent report and the DoF Minister’s response to that report.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for an update on the Review of the Minimum Standards for Childminding and Day Care; (ii) when the public consultation will be launched; and (ii) to detail the reason for the delay in publishing the consultation.
To ask the Minister of Education (i) to detail the amount of the £25 million designated for childcare interventions that has been spent to date; and (ii) to provide a breakdown of the spend.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to the answer to AQW 15273/22-27.The answer is contained in the Written Answers Booklet for week ending 27 September 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 16119/22-27, (i) whether the Schools Trauma Advisory and Referral Service is no longer taking referrals; and (ii) whether this service will be expanded.
answer_text: The Schools Trauma Advisory Referral Service (STARS) was available to schools for early advice and onwards referrals to the Education Psychology and other Education Authority (EA) services.The Department and the EA are currently seeking additional Asylum Seekers and Refugees (ASR) Dispersal funding from the Home Office to help develop alternative ways of providing trauma-informed training, advice and support to schools who require urgent early intervention.  This will involve provision of Assessment Trained Professionals to undertake Trauma Informed reviews of new arrivals and Trauma Informed Practice Training for Teachers and Pastoral Care Leads, as soon as possible after arrival.  It is anticipated that revised support arrangements will be in place during Spring 2025 pending funding being made available to DE and EA.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether there are any plans to improve the public realm of Moira town centre given the uneven pavement surfaces in the town.
answer_text: My Department works collaboratively with Councils to deliver Urban Regeneration projects, such as Public Realm Schemes, in urban towns and city centres, with a policy focus on urban settlements with a population of 5,000 or more.		Moira has a population of less than 5,000 residents so a Public Realm Scheme would fall under the remit of the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs.		Uneven pavement surfaces are the responsibility of Department for Infrastructure which is responsible for making sure pavements are free from trip hazards and other defects as far as reasonably practical for public safety reasons.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any contingency measures in place to ensure children receive hot meals in school despite staff vacancies.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has a number of contingencies in place when disruption to school meal provision occurs and these are considered in the following order:Meals can be transported from another school with a production kitchen if it has sufficient capacity;Prepared meals can brought into the school, providing there are sufficient staff and oven capacity available to heat/ serve the meals;A packed lunch service can be offered to all pupils, subject to the capacity of the external provider;Packed lunches can be provided for pupils entitled to free school meals only, subject to the capacity of the external provider.The EA continues to prioritise the preparation and serving of meals in a safe environment, meeting food safety regulations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) his Department's policy for street lighting in rural areas; and (ii) when this policy was last reviewed.
answer_text: My Department’s policy for the provision of street lighting in rural areas, which was established in 1993, is detailed in RSPPG E072 and can be accessed via the following link. Street lighting RSPPG E072 | Department for Infrastructure (infrastructure-ni.gov.uk)The policy uses two main criteria when considering the provision of street lighting in rural areas:		the density of housing within a community, including public buildings with significant night-time use; and 		road safety, where street lighting would contribute to a reduction in the number of night-time collisions.The policy is subject to continual informal review and ensures that the limited resources available for the provision of street lighting are directed to those areas where they can provide the maximum benefit to the public, particularly where road or infrastructure safety is concerned.It also serves to balance the demand for more rural lighting against the unwelcome effects of increased urbanisation of the countryside and the environmental impact of night sky light pollution on people, wildlife and fauna.As such, I have no immediate plans to change this policy at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he will commission a detailed review of capital costs required to standardise pre school education hours, given the current scoping report was based on self-assessment of capital requirements.
answer_text: The scoping study report was based on a range of data which included the self-assessed state of readiness provided by individual pre-school education settings as well as input from both my Department and the EA in regard to capital and minor works.The report provides an outline ten-year implementation plan. As the programme rolls out, I will consider the need for the data to be refreshed or supplemented as appropriate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the availability of educational psychologists.
answer_text: The Independent Review of Education (December 2023) considered the Education Authority’s (EA) Educational Psychology Service (EPS) and recommended that ‘the staffing of the Educational Psychology Service should return to the level of five or so years ago with around 130 fully qualified psychologists’.My department has invested in the Educational Psychology workforce and is currently funding 38 student places on the Doctorate in Educational, Child and Adolescent Psychology (DECAP) course at Queen’s University Belfast (QUB) which provides the qualification to enable registration as a practitioner.  Those completing the course are obliged to take up employment in the EA’s EPS for at least three years after they qualify.  Consequently, we have seen the staffing in EPS increase.There are currently 130 full-time equivalent (FTE) staff in the EPS (excluding administrative staff), comprised of 94.06 FTE Educational Psychologists and 35.87 FTE Assistant Educational Psychologists.  It is expected that the number of Educational Psychologists will continue to increase annually as students complete the DECAP course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when the initial list of pre-schools identified for transition to standardised hours will be announced.
answer_text: To ensure the programme progresses as quickly as possible, my Department will publish details of proposed settings in tranches. The Education Authority is currently engaging with individual pre-school settings to confirm their individual state of readiness.  It is the intention that this work will be completed in time for the details of the first tranche of proposed settings to be published for consultation before November 2024. Further information on the Standardisation Programme is available on the DE website at Pre-School Education Programme - Standardisation | Department of Education (education-ni.gov.uk) and this will be updated as the programme progresses.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) the number of schools that cannot currently provide hot meals due to catering staff shortages; (ii) the number of children impacted by this; and (iii) the number of children impacted by this that are entitled to free school meals.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that as of 11 October, there are two schools that cannot currently provide hot meals due to catering staff shortages.  The EA estimates that across these two schools, an average of 350 children would normally take a school meal each day and 51 of these pupils are entitled to free school meals.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any capital works are planned for Dromara to increase the capacity of the sewers servicing the village.
answer_text: The management of public wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised as follows:NI Water has no capital investment planned for Dromara within the current Price Control 21 period (2021/22 - 2026/27). A detailed appraisal study is required to assess the engineering options to address properties affected by internal flooding. NI Water plans to commence this study in 2025/26, subject to the availability of funding. Any capital investment options subsequently identified, will be considered during the company’s prioritisation process for its Price Control 27 Business Plan, which covers the period 2027/28 - 2032/33. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.comMaking direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the current job vacancy list for the Education Authority, including how long each job has been unfilled.
answer_text: The table below provides information on the Education Authority’s (EA) job vacancy list as at 11th October 2024. Information on the how long each role has been unfilled is not held centrally on the EA’s recruitment system and is therefore not readily available.  Education Authority job vacancy list as at 11th October 2024 Post TypeNumber of job vacanciesCorporate vacancies170Administrative35Architectural4Classroom and Playground Support4Finance3Human Resources6IT3Management4Operational95Procurement1School Support3Surveyor1Youth11School Based 123Administrative7Classroom and Playground Support68Education Welfare1Operational11School Support4Teaching32Grand Total293Notes:Information is based on vacant posts in the EA advertised as at 11 October 2024. The EA job vacancy list can change on a daily basis.The current EA vacancy list is available on the EA external and internal job sites.  These sites are updated and change daily. The responsibility for school-based staffing matters, including the management of vacancies, is delegated to individual schools. This means it is not possible to provide information on all vacancies across schools.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any actions he is taking to support schools that are experiencing catering job vacancies.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has been targeting its resources in order to recruit staff to fill catering vacancies.  The actions include the use of social media, school platforms, roadshow recruitment days and the Job page of the EA website.  The EA has also put in place a small mobile catering team to minimise disruption where possible.In addition, we have recently agreed a Pay and Grading review for education sector support staff.  This agreement will be implemented by the EA in the next few months and will increase the rates of pay available for roles within the catering service.  It is hoped that this will help address some of the recruitment and retention issues currently being faced by the EA, many of which are also being experienced across the wider catering and hospitality sectors.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the target time frame for NI Water to remove properties from the DG5 register.
answer_text: The management of the DG5 register is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and been advised that for the PC21 Price Control period (2021 – 2027) the target is to remove a total of 57 properties from the DG5 register.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department will commit to cover the healthcare travel costs for all cancer patients under the age of 25.
answer_text: I recognise that the unexpected costs for patients undergoing hospital treatment can add additional financial pressure at an already difficult time. There are existing schemes in place to help support individuals and their dependants in Northern Ireland who need access to healthcare. The Help with Healthcare Costs Scheme provides individuals and their dependants, primarily on low incomes, with financial help towards the cost of dental treatment, eyesight tests, glasses or contact lens, and travel costs for health and social care treatment on referral by a doctor or dentist. The eligibility criteria are set out in legislation and certain benefits automatically passport on to the scheme.  				There are currently no plans under development for an additional support scheme at this time. In the absence of an agreed multi-year budget for health and a significant projected overspend, such a scheme would not be currently affordable and would have to be considered in the future alongside the many other competing funding demands across the Health and Social Care system.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of properties on the DG5 register.
answer_text: The management of the DG5 register is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and been advised that there are currently 87 properties on the NI Water DG5 Internal Flooding Register.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department will introduce a Northern Ireland-specific drug tariff for pharmacies.
answer_text: Northern Ireland does have its own Drug Tariff for community pharmacies. Following the 2010 Judicial Review and subsequent consultation on improved arrangements, the then Health Minister decided that the English Drug Tariff should be used as the basis for reimbursement of medicines supplied with modifications for local NI circumstances such as services provided in NI. This policy position was re-affirmed by Minister Swann in May 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether there are plans to introduce electronic scripts for pharmacies.
answer_text: My Departments Strategic Planning and Performance Group leads on a programme to replace the current written prescription system with an electronic prescribing system. Whilst there is policy support for this programme my Department continues to operate under significant budgetary and financial constraints including in our Capital budget and timelines have not yet been confirmed.  I remain hopeful that the implementation of this project will be able to proceed once capital funding has been prioritised for this programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the data his Department is collecting from childcare providers, including childminders, through the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme.
answer_text: Data is provided by childcare providers when they register for the scheme as follows:-•	Provider name•	Contact details•	HSCT registration details•	Type of registration•	Provider type•	Management type•	Days open, open / closing time, weeks open annually•	Frequency of invoice to parents, start date for preparing invoices•	Fee structure •	Child level usage i.e. number of days attended per week•	Bank detailsProviders are also required to provide invoice details for each of the eligible children within their provision on a monthly basis.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the safeguarding checks that private tutors currently undergo.
answer_text: The role of private tutors is not overseen or regulated by my department.The Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups (Northern Ireland) Order 2007 set out the requirements for safeguarding checks, stipulating that vetting checks are required to be completed for individuals entering regulated occupations as defined in the legislation.  Such checks are undertaken by AccessNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether additional funding will be available to schools to support newcomer children where English is not their first language.
answer_text: Schools receive additional funding annually through the Common Funding Formula for each newcomer child enrolled at the October census.  A newcomer child is defined as a child who does not share a first language in common with their teachers and peers (i.e. English, or Irish for those children educated within Irish Medium schools).  In the 2023/24 financial year, the total additional funding amounted to circa £22m of funding to schools in support of newcomer children.In addition to newcomer pupil funding directed to schools, the Department also supports children arriving in Northern Ireland through a number of Government schemes, including those for the resettlement of Afghan and Syrian citizens, the Homes for Ukraine scheme and The Executive Office’s Dispersal fund for children seeking asylum and refuge.  In the current financial year a total of over £900k will be directed to schools and the Education Authority (EA) to remove barriers to education for this cohort of children and to ensure they are welcomed and succeeding within our education system.The EA’s Intercultural Education Service (IES) provide specific newcomer support to schools and families around the school placement process, including welfare supports such as Free School Meals, Uniform Grants and School Transport. This includes advice and guidance to families for children with Special Educational Needs and disability.In partnership with the EA’s Educational Psychology Service, IES operate the ‘Schools Trauma Advisory and Referral Service’ (STARS) which is open to all schools with newcomer pupils who are concerned about trauma as the main presenting barrier to learning and integration in school.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of actions led, or co-led, by his Department in a Fair Start Report (i) have been completed; (ii) are being progressed; and (iii) have yet to be actioned.
answer_text: Of the 47 actions in the original “A Fair Start” report, six have been completed and 30 are being progressed. This includes three additional actions identified as complementary to the actions within the report. 14 actions have yet to be completed. Since June 2021 when the report was first published, an additional six actions were identified and completed. Progress Reports can be found at https://www.education-ni.gov.uk/fair-start

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he is considering amendments to the Sex Discrimination (Northern Ireland) Order 1976 to align with the Worker Protection (Amendment of Equality Act 2010) Act 2023.
answer_text: The relevant provisions (sections 1-4) within the Worker Protection (Amendment of the Equality Act 2010) Act 2023 which reinforce the requirement for employers to tackle sexual harassment, will commence in England, Scotland and Wales on 26 October 2024.Amendments to the Sex Discrimination (Northern Ireland) Order 1976 would be necessary to ensure that the new requirements upon employers to tackle sexual harassment also apply in Northern Ireland.  My officials are considering the way forward on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the amount of funding provided to schools to facilitate and enable Specialist Provision in Mainstream Schools to date.
answer_text: Specialist Provision in Mainstream Schools (SPiMS) emerged in 2020 in response to the unprecedented increase in demand for specialist education.  However, legacy ‘learning support’ classes have existed for many years.  SPiMS are, and have always been, part of the continuum of special education provision for children and young people.The Education Authority (EA) does not provide funding to schools to facilitate SPiMS, rather they are funded directly via the EA’s central budget.  The total EA expenditure on the establishment and operation of SPiMS from 2020/21 (including projected expenditure for 2024/25) is £221.5m.  This does not include expenditure by Voluntary Grammar/Grant Maintained Integrated schools.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when the review of staff to child ratios across all preschool education settings, as referenced to in a Fair Start Report, will be published.
answer_text: In 2022 my Department commissioned a scoping study to analyse the current state of the pre-school education sector and cost a range of options for standardisation of funded pre-school education provision, including adjusting the staffing ratio. The report was circulated to Executive colleagues and is available on the Department’s website at Final Scoping Study Report | Department of Education (education-ni.gov.uk).

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of funding provided in 2024 to further education colleges to provide classes in English for people seeking asylum who speak another language.
answer_text: For the 2024/2025 financial year, the Department for the Economy has provided £1.7m funding for the provision of English for Speakers of Other Languages to Further Education colleges as part of their overall 2024/2025 resource budget allocation.  However, it is not possible to determine how much of this funding is specifically allocated to deliver classes to people seeking asylum who speak another language as the Department does not hold this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any actions he is taking to improve the adoption process.
answer_text: The Adoption and Children Act, which received Royal Assent on 27 April 2022 will reform the legislative framework and procedural arrangements governing adoption in Northern Ireland and will lead to the introduction of a range of new measures aimed at improving the adoption process. The Act will make the adoption process as efficient and robust as possible, reducing unnecessary delay and uncertainty for children. The framework for adoption will be more consistent with the principles and provisions of the Children (NI) Order 1995, the UN Convention on the Rights of the Child and international human rights, ensuring that the child is put firmly at the centre of the adoption process.The Act will introduce a statutory principle that, in general, delay is likely to prejudice the child’s welfare and a requirement for courts to draw up timetables for resolving adoption cases without delay. The aim is to reduce the drift and unnecessary delay that prolongs instability for children and families when adoption would be in a child’s best interests.The Act also has power to enable adoption services to be managed and/or provided on either a regional or sub-regional basis. It is considered that regionalising some aspects of the service could potentially provide greater consistency, efficiency and accountability, build expertise and contribute to a greater culture of cross-sectoral partnership.The Act will also place a duty on Trusts to make arrangements for the provision of adoption support services, as specified in regulations.  This duty will be used to deliver a framework for adoption support services at both placement and post adoption order.There is also a power to establish an independent review mechanism relating to adoption decision making which will be prescribed in regulations. This mechanism will enable prospective adopters to request a review of an adoption agency’s decision that they should not be approved to adopt.This is a substantive programme of work and implementation of the Act is being taken forward on a phased basis over a period of 5 years from 2022 to 2027. Implementation of the Act is currently progressing within the context of a severely restricted budget and social workforce challenges.  Provisions to enable the introduction of transitional adoption support services arrangements and extended support for care leavers have been delayed until April 2025.  Work is almost complete on the necessary regulations and guidance required to support implementation as soon as it is possible to do so.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he is planning to introduce a right to miscarriage leave for supply teachers.
answer_text: Teachers’ (including supply teachers’) terms & conditions are negotiated and agreed by the Teachers’ Negotiating Committee (TNC) which comprises representatives of teachers’ employers and sectoral bodies, the Department of Education and the five recognised teachers’ unions. Decisions are communicated to teachers and their employers via TNC Circulars.TNC 2021/1 details the Teachers’ Maternity Leave Scheme.This Scheme is the result of an agreement reached between the Management and Teachers' Sides of TNC. It reflects rules concerning statutory and occupational maternity leave as a consequence of the relevant legislation.The Scheme applies to all pregnant teachers irrespective of pay or weekly working hours. The Scheme includes provisions in the event of a miscarriage. Each case must be considered on its own merits.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the Maze/Long Kesh Development Corporation is constrained to matters of health and safety, site security, essential maintenance and supporting the current tenants in the absence of Ministerial agreement on the future use of the site.
answer_text: The Development Corporation is currently charged with maintaining and managing the site, carrying out essential health and safety works, and supporting the existing tenants.The Development Corporation seeks as far as possible to maintain a state of readiness for future development.  This includes continuing to consider possible approaches to regeneration of the site and provide advice and options for the future.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on plans to redevelop the former Dromore Central Primary School site.
answer_text: The former Dromore Primary School site has been retained and is being considered for SEN provision.  Initial technical assessments have been carried out and draft plans have been developed for extensive refurbishment works to the existing building.Unfortunately, timeframes cannot be provided at this time as works are subject to Business Case approvals being received as well as available funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for a breakdown of the (i) average time taken; and (ii) longest waiting time to process the family assessment to qualify for adoption in the last five years.
answer_text: At this time, the Department of Health does not capture the specific data required to provide the breakdown requested. Through the Directed Statutory Functions (DSF) report received annually from each HSC Trust, the information provided below details the length of time prospective adopters wait from their formal application to adopt to commencing their adoption assessment for the past five years. Time waiting31/3/2431/3/2331/3/2231/3/2131/3/20Less than 1 month571414<5More than 1 month, less than 3 months10<5111619More than 3 months, less than 6 months145128<5More than 6 months, less than 12 months<5522<5<51 year or more<5<5<5<5<5Note: <5 denotes a figure of between 1 and 5.  The actual figure is suppressed to avoid disclosureThe DSF reports from HSC Trusts detail that a significant number of assessments are delayed in commencing due to the availability of social work staff to progress these. Children’s social services teams within HSC Trusts have experienced significant pressures which have impacted on the capacity of the Trusts to discharge their statutory duties, including the assessment of prospective adoption families.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the development of a new green growth strategy.
answer_text: My Department has been leading the development of the Green Growth Strategy, on behalf of the Northern Ireland Executive, collaborating with other departments, local government and key stakeholders from across the business, voluntary and community sectors.The draft Green Growth Strategy is the Executive’s multi-decade strategy for climate, the environment & the economy.  Its aim is to ensure future government policy making here has climate and environmental action at its core.The draft strategy sets out a long-term vision for tackling the climate and environment challenge in a way that benefits all our people and with an emphasis on just and fair economic growth.  It proposes a range of principles and 10 commitments which will help balance climate action, clean environment and green jobs. In October 2021, the then Executive agreed the text of a draft Green Growth Strategy. The strategy was issued for an eight-week public consultation, revised and updated to incorporate feedback from the consultation exercise and the impact of the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) which received Royal Assent on 6 June 2022. While the updated strategy was agreed by the former DAERA Minister, it was not possible to secure approval in the absence of an Executive.In the interim, the draft Strategy has been further updated to ensure its accuracy and relevance. The strategy was issued to all Departments for review in July 2024, has been updated to reflect feedback and is being finalised prior to consideration by the Executive.I hope to take the Strategy to the Executive this autumn for approval to publish.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy what action he is taking to promote investment to and from the European Union and the European Economic Area to maximise any benefits from dual market access.

To ask the Minister of Education to detail the consultation his Department undertook with (i) children and young people; (ii) parents; and (iii) school leaders prior to his approval of a £250,000 capital spend on magnetic lockable pouches for mobile phones in schools.
answer_text: Officials are preparing a business case for the pilot scheme and an associated specification for the procurement process.  In advance of the roll-out of the pilot there will be engagement with pupils, parents and school leaders to inform the evaluation process.The guidance provided to schools is informed by relevant evidence that highlights the impact of the unauthorised use of mobile devices in school, as well as the excessive use of such devices more generally.   The guidance sets out a range of different approaches that could be used in schools to restrict the use of mobile phones, this includes the potential use of inhibiting devices.  The circular asks schools to revise existing policies accordingly to develop approaches that work for their own environment. As part of this, it is highlighted that schools should consult with the wider school community, including children and young people as well as parents on the specific delivery of policies at school level.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he will commit to deliver the recommendations set out in the UK Blood Cancer Action Plan.
answer_text: My Department acknowledges the recommendations outlined within the report and how these can help to deliver better outcomes for those living with blood cancer. However, my Department was not consulted or involved in the development of this particular publication.My Department has undertaken service reviews across the core haematology cancer pathways (Myeloma, Lymphoma and Leukaemia) in Northern Ireland. The haematology pathway reviews have identified a range of recommendations that will help transform the future delivery of haematology services. Work is ongoing to progress the implementation of the haematology review recommendations and is dependent on the complexity and scale of the review recommendations, available resources and other existing commissioning priorities.I remain fully committed to the implementation of the NI Cancer Strategy and to working collaboratively with all stakeholders to transform our cancer services that will deliver better outcomes for all of those impacted by a cancer diagnosis in Northern Ireland.
